checks.finding: requirements.md REQ-002/T-002 lists two 'marker' fixture categories: (i) 'an abstract base class with legitimate raise NotImplementedError methods' and (ii) 'an existing, task-unrelated # TODO marker in a file the fixture never lists as changed.' Only category (ii) is given an explicit 'never lists as changed' rule, repeated verbatim in the Edge Cases section - the NotImplementedError file gets no equivalent explicit statement anywhere, even though the same T-002 bullet's test-lock description says the changed-files set for case (a) excludes 'the marker-bearing files' (plural) and AC-008/AC-009 both say 'exits 0 despite the seed's pre-existing markers' (plural). A downstream test/task author cannot determine from the spec alone whether the NotImplementedError abstract-base-class file must also be excluded from AC-008's 'changed files' input, or may legitimately be included as ordinary changed content: one implementer would exclude it, another would include it, yielding different fixture composition and a different observable AC-008 pass/fail outcome against the real check-placeholders scan. This is a gap in the acceptance-criteria's own input-composition contract for a named behavior-lock test (AC-008/AC-009), not an implementation-architecture choice. Severity Major: it changes what the AC-008 lock case demonstrates without rising to a direct contradiction.

No direct conflict found among requirements, acceptance criteria, constraints, or non-goals. The round-1 AC-003/AC-005 rewrite holds together: Non-goals states AC-005's real-document smoke check 'can only exercise the attempt < 3 direction' while AC-003's synthetic sweep alone exercises the Audit-Attempt>=3/Human-Blocked direction - disjoint, compatible scopes. The AC-014 cross-reference disambiguation is applied identically everywhere it appears, and this feature's own AC-014 is never conflated with it. Roles and Permissions' protected-gate claim matches Non-goals' explicit protected-file exclusion list.

acceptance-tests.md represents the material negative/boundary paths implied by requirements.md's Edge Cases section: TEST-002 is the mandatory negative-branch canary for the HITL leg; TEST-003 embeds a negative self-check mutating the WFI-audit threshold in a temp copy; TEST-004 is a construction-proof negative-space check (no gh invocation, Category never plugin-improvement); TEST-008/TEST-009 form the explicit positive/negative pair locking check-placeholders' changed-files-only vs. full-directory scan; TEST-006 includes a threshold-0 negative self-check. The exact convergence-guard boundary value (Audit-Attempt=3) is the value AC-003 asserts transitions to Human-Blocked.

All seven investigation.md Open Questions (OQ-1..OQ-7) are marked RESOLVED, and each resolution is folded into requirements.md's own text rather than left as an investigation-only claim: Field Definitions closes OQ-2/OQ-5/OQ-6; Non-goals closes OQ-1's directional scope for TEST-005; REQ-002/AC-007 closes OQ-3/OQ-4; REQ-003/REQ-004 close OQ-6/OQ-7. The four listed Assumptions are each either verified against cited evidence or explicitly flagged as an accepted out-of-scope residual risk.

No feature-specific human-approval, governance, or irreversible-change boundary is in scope. Roles and Permissions' human-maintainer approval of specs/tasks is the generic SDD pipeline gate applicable to every feature, not a boundary this specification itself defines. Security Boundaries B1-B4 and Non-goals rule out any irreversible action: no gh invocation reachable from any new file (B4, AC-004), no write to the real docs/workflow-improvements/ or specs/ trees (B1, AC-005's SHA-256 before/after immutability assertion), no gate weakened or reimplemented (B2). Test-infrastructure and precheck-script parity work with no production-state-changing surface, so the check's explicit skip condition applies.

The specification carries file:line evidence for essentially every acceptance criterion, and Field Definitions resolves the terms a design reviewer would otherwise have to invent. Every investigation Open Question is closed in-document rather than deferred. The one residual Major ambiguity flagged under AMBIGUITY (NotImplementedError-file changed-files-list membership) is narrow - a single test-fixture composition detail inside one acceptance criterion (AC-008) - and does not require the design reviewer to invent broader missing product behavior.
